evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` builds five nested pipeline objects (`dropPipeline`, `duplicatePipeline`, `tapToMovePipeline`, `structureVideoPipeline`, `endpointPipeline`) and forwards them into `useTimelineActionHandlers`, which mostly re-destructures and re-forwards values.

`useSegmentSlotMode` combines routing/deep-link synchronization, pair-data computation, frame-update debouncing, structure-video overlap policy, and lightbox view-model construction in one 593-line hook.

Edge handlers show design drift despite shared infrastructure: e.g. `revoke-pat` constructs `new SystemLogger(...)` instead of using bootstrap logger, while sibling handlers use `bootstrap.value.logger`; response contracts are mixed (`jsonResponse` vs raw `Response` text) across similar endpoints.

findings.summary: Timeline orchestration relies on oversized pipeline-bag interfaces that add indirection without clear ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` assembles large nested pipeline objects and passes them to `useTimelineActionHandlers` instead of composing focused hooks directly.
`useTimelineActionHandlers` destructures many fields and mostly forwards them into `useUnifiedDrop`, `useTapToMove`, and `useEndpointDrag`, indicating a pass-through abstraction layer.
Viewport and action controller hooks each require broad input bags, increasing cross-hook coupling and making changes require synchronized edits in multiple wrappers.
findings.suggestion: Replace pipeline-bag inputs with smaller concern-specific hooks invoked directly in the orchestrator (drop, duplicate, tap-to-move, endpoint drag). Keep each hook contract minimal and localize ownership so adding/removing one concern does not force shape changes across all orchestrator layers.

findings.summary: Segment slot mode hook mixes navigation state machine, domain policy, and UI view-model assembly in one module.
evidence: `useSegmentSlotMode` includes deep-link routing effects (`useLocation`/`useNavigate`), state reset choreography, pair/trailing slot synthesis, structure video overlap resolution, and debounced frame update logic.
The same hook also constructs `SegmentSlotModeData` with many callbacks and rendering-facing fields, coupling domain computation and presentation concerns.
Lightbox-related navigation/state composition is handled in separate shared hooks elsewhere (`useSharedLightboxState`), showing uneven boundary decisions for similar orchestration responsibilities.
findings.suggestion: Split `useSegmentSlotMode` into: (1) URL/deep-link state synchronizer, (2) segment-pair domain calculator, and (3) presentation adapter that maps domain state to `SegmentSlotModeData`. Keep overlap-resolution policy in a standalone pure module with tests.

findings.summary: Supabase edge handlers follow inconsistent runtime/logger/response conventions, weakening a shared handler architecture.
evidence: `revoke-pat` creates `new SystemLogger(...)` after bootstrap instead of using the logger returned by `bootstrapEdgeHandler`, unlike sibling handlers.
Sibling endpoints return different response envelope styles for similar validation/auth failures (`jsonResponse` JSON vs raw text `Response`).
Shared request/runtime utilities exist in `_shared/edgeRequest.ts`, but endpoint implementations still diverge in method/error contract patterns.
findings.suggestion: Enforce a single edge handler contract: always consume bootstrap logger/runtime, standardize error envelopes, and route response creation through shared helpers. Add a lightweight endpoint template or lint check to prevent logger/response drift in new functions.

findings.summary: Task-parameter extraction and precedence logic are duplicated across edge and app layers with no shared contract authority.
evidence: `complete_task/params.ts` maintains many path-fallback extractors (`extractFromParams`, `extractShotAndPosition`, `extractBasedOn`) for evolving payload shapes.
Client task builders in `individualTravelSegment.ts` and `imageGeneration.ts` maintain separate precedence/normalization logic (`resolveByPrecedence`, parser helpers, mode-specific filtering).
The same domain concepts (shot IDs, orchestrator refs, optional field precedence) are encoded in multiple places, raising drift risk when payload shape changes.
findings.suggestion: Define a versioned shared task-params contract (schema + generated accessors) consumed by both edge functions and client task builders. Centralize fallback path lists and precedence rules in one module/package and deprecate ad hoc extractors.
